ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Continued To Apr-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Continued To Mar-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Wood for Paul Kitagawa. Mr. Wood's assistant, Harry Dixon, is also present.

The Court noted it is not aware of any objections but that there are a number of issues such as one of the heirs taking assets, the receipt of behalf of Eliot was signed by someone else, and an insurance policy that did not pass through the estate. Mr. Wood stated that the life insurance was paid directly to the beneficiaries and that the estate was not the beneficiary, that he sent the papers from the court to the insurance company and the insurance company wanted to pay the beneficiaries directly. Mr. Wood further spoke of the family dynamics between the decedent and his 8 children, that he mis-read the code and should have petitioned for a preliminary distribution, that he wanted to send checks to the children who were still competent and to two who have special needs trusts. The Court noted that if they want to deposit into special needs trust accounts, they will need to amend the petition. Mr. Wood stated that all the funds are in a bank in Marin that require the signature of the Administrator and himself, discuss the issues of attorney's costs, statutory fees, executor's fees, and stated that Eliot did not want to sign the receipt but he has the cleared check. The Court inquired if 2017 taxes have been paid and what $5,000.00 reserve is for. Mr. Wood stated all taxes have been paid, that the government thinks it gave a refund in error, that the reserve is for anything that might come up for another 6 months, and he doesn't know if there is going to be anything else.

The First And Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc., is continued to March 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to file an amended petition, provide notice for March 24, 2020 hearing date, address the issue of receipt for Eliot, and take another look at how much reserve is requested.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-26-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Continued To Jun-26-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Robert Wood for Paul Kitagawa.

Mr. Wood stated that he has not filed a final petition, that he is waiting for the State of California to provide satisfaction of their creditor's claim, that he is waiting for federal transcripts to see if the Decedent filed taxes for 2015, 2016 and 2017, and that there is a little bit of litigation going on because one of the Beneficiaries stole $100,000.00 shortly after the Father's death. Status on close of estate is continued to June 26, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Mr. Wood that either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has to be filed at least three Court days prior.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Continued To May-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Wood for Paul Kitagawa (Petitioner)

The Court noted the petitioner requested the petition be granted without bond, but a beneficiary has not filed a waiver of bond in proper form. Mr. Wood stated that one of the beneficiaries is a ward of adult protective services, that the ward and his case worker agreed to the waiver of bond, but someone higher up told them not to sign the waiver because it is a contract. Mr. Wood also stated that the only assets are 2 blocked bank accounts, are non-appraisal assets, and request to waive Probate Referee. The Court clarified that the beneficiary is a Conservatee, stated it will not receive a waiver that is not on a judicial council form, and the only options are to proceed with bond or continue the matter to allow Counsel time to file a waiver of bond. Mr. Woods accepts bond requirement. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $180,000 is required. The status on close of estate is set for May 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
